reviewer: build wave — Llama 3.2 3B WebLLM (runner-evidenced + both screenshots read + honest needs-WebGPU verified on a no-GPU runner)

rubric.evidence: conformance (webllm, headless no-GPU): loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass (source uses createModelLoader + worker → lib/webllm.js createEngine/streamChat → CreateMLCEngine). webgpu-capability-honest=BLOCKED = the device genuinely has no GPU adapter, so the page correctly shows its needs-WebGPU state instead of tokens (NOT a fail, NOT a fake). Runtime path is real; token generation is only reachable on a WebGPU device. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/llama-3.2-webllm/{desktop,mobile}.png + /tmp/w13d-runit.png (agent-read).
rubric.notes: WebLLM is WebGPU-only with no WASM fallback; on this no-GPU runner the honest capability state is the correct real-inference outcome. createEngine/streamChat wiring verified in worker.js + lib/webllm.js; MLC id Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC verified present in prebuiltAppConfig (vram 2263.69 MB, ctx 4096).

rubric.evidence: io-shape-semantic=manual (WebGPU device required to stream tokens). Source path streams delta chunks into the chat bubble via streamChat's onToken; See-inside stats (tok/s, ttft, decode, generated, context) are all derived from the REAL res.ms/res.ttft/res.chunks of a run — no hardcoded numbers (all render '–' until a real run). Practical page additionally parses the JSON tool call and validates tool ∈ known set. screenshots read.
rubric.notes: Expected output: streamed chat continuation (basics/wild), strict JSON tool call {tool, arguments} (practical, response_format json_object), grounded cited answer (multi-model). Semantic sanity awaits an agent verdict on a GPU device.

rubric.evidence: conformance (webllm, headless no-GPU): declares-model-id=pass (meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct linked), declares-task=pass (text-generation), declares-backend=pass (WebGPU), declares-quantisation=pass (q4f16_1), inference-off-main-thread=pass (new Worker(worker.js)). screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/llama-3.2-webllm/{desktop,mobile}.png
rubric.notes: At-a-glance table states the exact MLC build id, 3.21B params, q4f16_1, WebGPU-only, ~1.9 GB download / ~2.3 GB GPU memory, ctx 4096, Llama 3.2 Community License — all grounded in the real model card + prebuiltAppConfig.

rubric.evidence: conformance: shared-auto-init-loader=pass (createModelLoader), auto-init-on-cached=manual. On the no-GPU runner the loader detail line reads 'Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC · webllm · ~1900 MB · 0 MB cached of 10737 MB' (agent-read /tmp/w13d-runit.png) — fresh profile ⇒ correctly cache-absent, no silent download. Uses the shared model-loader/model-cache architecture verbatim, so cached auto-init + Clear-cached-model match the built llama-3-2-1b-webllm behaviour.
rubric.notes: No hand-rolled Load button; the WebGPU gate happens inside createModelLoader (requiresWebGPU:true) before any cache work.

rubric.evidence: conformance (webllm, headless no-GPU): live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live), errors-on-the-page=pass (.status.err + Retry path in loader; per-page catch surfaces 'Generation failed: …'), honest-progress-retry-offline=manual, webgpu-capability-honest=BLOCKED (honest needs-WebGPU panel rendered — agent-read). screenshots read.
rubric.notes: The needs-WebGPU fallback panel (webgpuFallbackHTML) gives the real reason + enable steps + the ~1.9 GB / ~2.3 GB cost + a link to the lighter 1B page. No blank panel, no faked reply.

rubric.evidence: controls-operable=manual. Agent-read screenshots: temperature/top_p/max-tokens sliders with live value read-outs, system-prompt textarea, Send/Stop/Clear buttons (main page); Route-to-a-tool + sample chips with aria-pressed (practical); setting chips + Begin/Act (wild); dual loaders + Ask (multi-model). Every control is a real labelled <button>/<input>/<textarea>; Enter-to-send + Shift+Enter newline wired.
rubric.notes: Sample/persona/setting chips are real buttons with aria-pressed state and min-block-size 32px.

rubric.evidence: No canned output path anywhere: every visible answer/stat is produced from the worker's streamChat result, and on a no-GPU device the page shows the honest needs-WebGPU state instead (agent-read /tmp/w13d-runit.png — See-inside stats all '–', chat log shows only the placeholder, no tokens). No hardcoded tokens/tok-s.
rubric.notes: Task mandate 'honest needs-WebGPU state headless (NO fake tokens)' satisfied — verified.

rubric.evidence: conformance (webllm, headless no-GPU): images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. Live regions (role=status aria-live) for status + chat log; sample/persona chips are labelled buttons with aria-pressed; :focus-visible via shared styles. screenshots read.

responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean (no horizontal overflow, no clipped controls, clean console + network) on 1280×800 + 360×740 (DPR3). Both screenshots read: header/table/controls reflow, chat log + composer stack on mobile, sliders wrap. responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass, parity-both-classes=manual. support seeded needs-review (automated) → agent matrix pass sets ok/ok.
rubric.notes: Chat/composer use flex-wrap + field-sizing:content textareas (min/max block-size, width:100%); glance table + grids use auto-fit minmax so they stack cleanly on narrow mobile.

rubric.evidence: conformance: inp-stays-low=manual; inference-off-main-thread=pass. All generation + the MiniLM embedding run in Web Workers (models/llama-3.2-webllm/worker.js + multi-model/embed-worker.js); the main thread only appends streamed deltas and reads sliders. No blocking loop. screenshots read.
rubric.notes: Token append is incremental per chunk; the See-inside compositing is O(1) per turn.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel no horizontal overflow chat composer
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Flexbox/grid with flex-wrap, min-inline-size:0 on flex items, auto-fit minmax grids, logical properties + intrinsic sizing instead of fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — chat-wrap/composer/controls use flex-wrap + flex:1 1 auto; glance table, tool-router and RAG panels use grid auto-fit minmax(260px,1fr) so they go two-up on desktop and stack on mobile. Result: desktop clean · mobile clean, no horizontal overflow at 360×740.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use field-sizing:content on textareas with min/max-block-size and an explicit width:100% to avoid jarring horizontal reflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — CHAT_CSS composer/system-prompt textareas use field-sizing:content with min/max-block-size and width:auto/100%, so the composer grows with typed input without breaking layout.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: webllm-chat.js CHAT_CSS .composer textarea / .sysbox textarea; screenshots show stable composer width

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status announcement keyboard operable controls
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Semantic live regions for status, labelled keyboard-operable controls, visible focus, aria-pressed for toggle chips.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — loader + page status use role=status aria-live=polite; chat log is aria-live; every control is a real labelled button/input; sample/persona/setting chips are buttons with aria-pressed; Enter/Shift+Enter keyboard handling on composers.

guidanceConsulted.query: stream chat tokens off main thread without INP regression
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy inference off the main thread (Web Worker), stream incrementally, avoid main-thread blocking loops so INP stays low.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all WebLLM generation runs in worker.js and MiniLM embedding in embed-worker.js; the main thread only appends streamed deltas and updates read-outs. No synchronous decode loop on the UI thread.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; source uses new Worker(...) for both models

openQuestions: On a real GPU, how much does the 3B's higher schema-adherence actually reduce tool-router JSON failures vs the 1B? Worth an A/B note once measured on-device.
The 3B may fail to allocate on GPUs with <3 GB free — should the fallback detect the allocation error specifically and recommend the 1B more prominently?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a size-comparison demo that runs the same prompt through the 1B and 3B side by side (both WebLLM) to make the capability/latency trade tangible.
On a WebGPU device, capture a real-inference agent verdict (streamed tokens + tool-call JSON sample) and bump the io-shape/real-inference evidence from manual to confirmed.

summary: Build-wave critique for llama-3.2-webllm (Llama 3.2 3B Instruct, text-generation, WebLLM/MLC, Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC — verified in prebuiltAppConfig). A genuinely distinct BIGGER sibling of the built llama-3-2-1b-webllm: 3B model, tool-router practical + game-master wild ladder. Real WebLLM createEngine/streamChat wiring off the main thread; on the no-GPU runner it shows the honest needs-WebGPU state (NO fake tokens) with webgpu-capability-honest=blocked. Conformance 14✓ 0✗ 1▨ 8◍/23; responsive desktop clean · mobile clean (both screenshots read). Guidance applied: css-layout, form-fields-automatically-fit-contents, accessibility, performance.
